LAW AND MOTION, PETITIONER JOCELYN WICKER'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE IS OFF CALENDAR WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED. MS. WICKER DID NOT FILE A PROOF OF SERVICE SHOWING THAT SHE SERVED HER MOVING PAPERS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 1005. PLAINTIFF IS ALSO TO SERVE THE MAYOR'S OFFICE. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandat
Off Calendar Nov-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jocelyn Wicker Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Margaret Baumgartner, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3800 for Defendant San Francisco.

RULING - A R G U E D. The matter is passed for Plaintiff to look at an order. The matter is recalled at 9:51 am. Both sides argue. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER JOCELYN WICKER's Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate.

Taken Under submission.*** Defendant submits a proposed order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

***After the hearing, the Court signs an Order denying Petitioner's writ of administrative mandate. (see scanned order for details)***

Notice Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jocelyn Wicker, Plaintiff appearing in pro per;
Atty. Margaret W. Baumgartner, Deputy City Attorney, Tel. No. 1-415-554-3859 specially
appearing for the Defendant CCSF.

RULING - The matter was discussed regarding service. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioner Jocelyn Wicker's petition for writ of administrative mandate is off calendar without prejudice to being refiled. Ms. Wicker did not file a proof of service showing that she served her moving papers in compliance with CCP 1005. Plaintiff is also to
serve the mayor's office. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
